summary: 1 (jeden) behaves like an ADJECTIVE — full gender agreement, matches noun's case and number
2-4 (dva/tri/štyri) behave like ADJECTIVES too — limited gender, noun stays in nominative plural
5+ (päť, šesť...) behave like NOUNS — the number itself is the 'subject', noun drops to genitive plural
Think: 'jeden muž' (one man) vs 'dva muži' (two men) vs 'päť mužov' (five OF men)
In oblique cases (G/D/L/I), all numbers agree with the noun's case — the special rules only apply in Nom/Acc
jeden: jeden/jedna/jedno — declines exactly like ten/tá/to
dva: dvaja (M personal) / dva (M other) / dve (F/N) — oblique: dvoch/dvom/dvoma
tri/štyri: traja/štyria (personal) / tri/štyri (other) — oblique: troch/trom/troma, štyroch/štyrom/štyrmi
Compound 21-99: only the units part declines, noun follows the units rule
sto (100) and tisíc (1000) are mostly indeclinable in everyday speech
Collective numerals (dvoje, troje, obaja/obe) exist for groups and pairs

rules.title: The Core Idea: Adjective → Adjective → Noun
rules.text: Slovak cardinals fall into three groups based on how they relate to the noun they modify. This is the single most important thing to understand.

rows: 'two men' — number still describes the noun
'a five of men' — number IS the subject, noun is 'of X'

examples: jeden muž pracuje — One man works (verb agrees with 'muž')
dva muži pracujú — Two men work (verb agrees with 'muži')
päť mužov pracuje — Five men works (verb agrees with 'päť', which is neuter singular!)
This is why 'päť mužov prišlo' uses neuter 'prišlo' — the subject is the number, not the men

rules.title: In Oblique Cases, Everything Agrees
rules.text: The adjective/noun distinction only matters in Nominative and Accusative. In all other cases (Genitive, Dative, Locative, Instrumental), the number and noun simply share the same case. This makes oblique cases much simpler.
examples: s jedným mužom / s dvoma mužmi / s piatimi mužmi — all instrumental, all agree
o jednom dome / o dvoch domoch / o piatich domoch — all locative, all agree

rules.title: jeden (1) — Full Adjective Declension
rules.text: Declines like an adjective with full gender agreement. Masculine accusative distinguishes animate (jedného) vs inanimate (jeden).
examples: Jeden muž, jedna žena, jedno dieťa
Vidím jedného muža (animate) vs Vidím jeden dom (inanimate)
S jednou ženou — With one woman

rules.title: dva (2) — Three-Way Gender Split
rules.text: Nominative/Accusative distinguish three forms: dvaja (M personal), dva (M non-personal), dve (F/N). All other cases are the same for all genders.
examples: Dvaja muži (two men — personal), Dva domy (two houses), Dve ženy (two women)
S dvoma mužmi / S dvoma ženami — same form for all genders in instrumental

rules.title: tri (3) and štyri (4) — Personal vs Non-personal
rules.text: Only Nominative and Accusative distinguish personal (traja/štyria) from non-personal (tri/štyri). All other cases are uniform. Note: štyri uses -mi in instrumental (not -ma like dva/tri).
examples: Traja študenti (personal) vs Tri autá (non-personal)
S troma mužmi vs So štyrmi mužmi (note: -ma vs -mi)

rules.title: 5+ — The 'Noun' Numbers
rules.text: Numbers 5 and above have NO gender distinction. Nominative and Accusative are the same (unchanged). Oblique cases follow a simple pattern: -ich (G/L), -im (D), -imi (I). The noun always takes genitive plural in Nom/Acc.
examples: Päť mužov prišlo — Five men came (noun in genitive plural, verb neuter singular)
S piatimi mužmi — With five men
O desiatich knihách — About ten books

rules.title: 11-20 and Tens (20-90)
rules.text: 11-19 are formed with -násť, tens with -dsať/-desiat. All decline exactly like 5+ with the -ich/-im/-imi pattern. The noun always takes genitive plural.
examples: Dvanásť mužov — Twelve men
S dvanástimi mužmi — With twelve men
Mám tridsaťpäť rokov — I am 35 years old

rules.title: Compound Numbers 21-99
rules.text: Tens + units joined together. Only the units part declines. The noun follows the rule of the units digit: 1 → singular, 2-4 → plural, 5-9 → genitive plural.
examples: Dvadsaťjeden muž — 21 men (singular! because 'jeden')
Dvadsaťdva muži — 22 men (plural because 'dva')
Dvadsaťpäť mužov — 25 men (genitive plural because 'päť')
Mám tridsaťpäť rokov — I am 35 years old (genitive plural)

rules.title: Time, Age, and Measurements
rules.text: Cardinals follow the same 1/2-4/5+ rules in time and measurement expressions.

rules.title: Collective Numerals
rules.text: Special forms for groups, pairs, and kinds. Used with pluralia tantum nouns (nouns that only exist in plural) and mixed groups.
examples: Dvoje topánok — Two pairs of shoes
Obaja bratia prišli — Both brothers came
